schemas.name: Rent vs Buy Calculator

schemas.headline: Rent vs Buy Calculator: The True Cost of Homeownership vs Renting
schemas.description: Think throwing away money on rent is bad? The shocking truth about unrecoverable homeownership costs might change your mind. Find your break-even point now.

mainEntity.name: Should I rent or buy a home?

acceptedAnswer.text: Whether you should rent or buy depends on your timeline, local market, and opportunity costs. Buying typically requires staying in the home for at least 5-7 years to offset high closing costs and initial interest-heavy mortgage payments. Use our rent vs buy calculator to find your exact break-even point.

mainEntity.name: Is it cheaper to rent or buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is often cheaper to rent in the short term, but cheaper to buy in the long term. This is because renting gives you lower upfront costs and no maintenance fees, allowing you to invest the difference. Over decades, however, buying generally builds more wealth through appreciation and equity.

mainEntity.name: How does a rent vs buy calculator work?

acceptedAnswer.text: A rent vs buy calculator compares the total unrecoverable costs of renting (monthly rent) against the unrecoverable costs of homeownership (mortgage interest, property taxes, maintenance, HOA, insurance) minus home appreciation. It also factors in the opportunity cost of investing your down payment elsewhere.

mainEntity.name: What are the unrecoverable costs of buying?

acceptedAnswer.text: When buying a house, not all your money goes to building wealth. Unrecoverable costs include property taxes, homeowners insurance, HOA fees, home maintenance, closing costs, and the interest portion of your mortgage payments.

mainEntity.name: Why is opportunity cost important in rent vs buy decisions?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you rent, you can take the money you would have used for a down payment and invest it in the stock market. The return on that investment is your opportunity cost. If the stock market outperforms your home's appreciation, renting might actually make you wealthier.
